title: A New Name for a Complex Condition: Why Experts are Renaming PCOS
subtitle: After 14 years of global research, experts hope a new name will lead to better care and understanding for millions of women.

summary.en: A common health condition previously known as PCOS is being renamed to PMOS. Experts say the old name was misleading because it focused only on the ovaries. The new name reflects that the condition affects the whole body, including hormone levels and metabolism. This change follows over a decade of international collaboration and patient feedback. The goal is to improve diagnosis and provide better support for those affected.
summary.zh: 一種先前被稱為 PCOS 的常見健康疾病正更名為 PMOS。專家表示，舊名稱因僅聚焦於卵巢而具有誤導性。新名稱反映了該疾病會影響全身，包括荷爾蒙濃度與代謝功能。這項變更是經過十多年國際合作與患者回饋後的結果。其目標是改善診斷並為受影響者提供更好的支持。

paragraphs.en: For many years, millions of women around the world have been diagnosed with a condition called polycystic ovary syndrome, or PCOS. However, after 14 years of global research and collaboration, experts have decided to change the name. The condition will now be known as polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome, or PMOS. This change was announced at the European Congress of Endocrinology in Prague and published in the medical journal The Lancet.
paragraphs.zh: 多年來，全球有數百萬名女性被診斷出患有一種稱為「多囊性卵巢症候群」（PCOS）的疾病。然而，經過十四年的全球研究與合作，專家們決定更改其名稱。該疾病現在將被稱為「多內分泌代謝卵巢症候群」（PMOS）。這項變更是在布拉格舉行的歐洲內分泌學大會上宣布，並刊登於醫學期刊《刺胳針》（The Lancet）。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, this condition affects one in eight women worldwide, totaling about 170 million people. The name change is not just a simple label update; it is a major effort to change how doctors and patients understand the illness. Prof Helena Teede, who led the project at the Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation, explained that the old name was misleading. She noted that it incorrectly focused only on the ovaries, which often led to delayed diagnoses and poor medical care.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，這種疾病影響全球八分之一的女性，總計約一億七千萬人。這次更名不僅僅是簡單的標籤更新，更是一項旨在改變醫師與患者對該疾病理解的重大努力。領導莫納許健康研究與執行中心（Monash Centre for Health Research and Implementation）該項計畫的蒂德教授（Prof Helena Teede）解釋說，舊名稱具有誤導性。她指出，舊名稱錯誤地將焦點僅放在卵巢上，這往往導致診斷延誤與醫療照護品質不佳。

paragraphs.en: To understand why the name is changing, it helps to look at what the condition actually is. The old name, PCOS, comes from the term "polycystic ovaries." Many people believed this meant there were cysts—small, fluid-filled sacs that can be painful or dangerous—on the ovaries. However, as Prof Colin Duncan from the University of Edinburgh explained to The Guardian, this is not true. What doctors see on an ultrasound are actually follicles, which are structures that contain an egg. In women with this condition, these follicles stop developing before they can release an egg. Because they do not mature, they stay in the ovary, making it look like there are many cysts when there are not.
paragraphs.zh: 為了理解為何要更改名稱，我們有必要了解該疾病的本質。舊名稱 PCOS 源自「多囊性卵巢」一詞。許多人認為這意味著卵巢上有囊腫——即可能引起疼痛或危險的充滿液體的小囊。然而，正如愛丁堡大學（University of Edinburgh）的鄧肯教授（Prof Colin Duncan）向《衛報》解釋的那樣，這並非事實。醫師在超音波檢查中看到的其實是濾泡，也就是含有卵子的結構。在患有此症的女性體內，這些濾泡在釋放卵子前就停止發育。由於它們沒有成熟，因此留在卵巢中，使其看起來像是長了許多囊腫，但事實並非如此。

paragraphs.en: Experts say the condition is much broader than just the ovaries. It is a hormonal and metabolic disorder. A key part of the condition involves an imbalance of hormones, which are the body's chemical messengers. For example, many people with the condition have high levels of androgens, often called "male sex hormones." While all women have these, having too many can cause symptoms like acne, extra facial or body hair, and irregular periods. Furthermore, about 85% of people with the condition also deal with insulin resistance. Insulin is a hormone that controls how the body uses sugar. When the body is resistant to insulin, it produces more of it, which in turn causes the ovaries to produce even more androgens. This creates a difficult cycle that can lead to weight gain and increase the risk of other health issues, such as diabetes and heart disease.
paragraphs.zh: 專家表示，這種疾病的影響範圍遠比卵巢廣泛，它是一種荷爾蒙與代謝失調。該疾病的關鍵部分涉及荷爾蒙失衡，而荷爾蒙是人體的化學信使。例如，許多患者體內的雄性素（通常被稱為「男性荷爾蒙」）濃度偏高。雖然所有女性體內都有雄性素，但含量過高會導致痤瘡、臉部或身體毛髮過多以及月經不規律等症狀。此外，約 85% 的患者同時面臨胰島素阻抗的問題。胰島素是一種控制身體如何利用糖分的荷爾蒙。當身體對胰島素產生阻抗時，會分泌更多胰島素，進而導致卵巢產生更多的雄性素。這形成了一種惡性循環，可能導致體重增加，並增加罹患糖尿病與心臟病等其他健康問題的風險。

paragraphs.en: For patients, the old name often caused confusion. Maddy Mavrikis, who was diagnosed at 15, shared her experience with The Guardian. Her doctor told her she might never have children and insisted she had cysts, even though her tests showed none. She spent years trying to understand a condition that her own doctors could not clearly explain. Mavrikis noted that her mother, who works in the medical field, often questioned why the name focused on ovaries when the symptoms were clearly hormonal. The new name, PMOS, is designed to fix this confusion by highlighting that the condition affects the entire endocrine system—the system that manages hormones—as well as the body's metabolism.
paragraphs.zh: 對於患者而言，舊名稱常造成困惑。十五歲時被診斷出此症的馬夫里基斯（Maddy Mavrikis）與《衛報》分享了她的經歷。她的醫師告訴她可能永遠無法生育，並堅稱她患有囊腫，儘管她的檢查結果顯示並無囊腫。她花了多年時間試圖理解一個連醫師都無法清楚解釋的疾病。馬夫里基斯提到，身為醫護人員的母親經常質疑，既然症狀明顯與荷爾蒙有關，為何名稱要聚焦在卵巢上。新名稱 PMOS 旨在解決這種困惑，強調該疾病影響的是整個內分泌系統（即管理荷爾蒙的系統）以及身體的代謝功能。

paragraphs.en: Experts believe that the new name will help doctors see the "multi-system burden" that patients face. By moving away from the focus on ovaries, the medical community hopes to recognize that this is a complex, whole-body condition. While researchers are still studying exactly why this happens, they believe it is a mix of genetics and environmental factors. Some studies suggest that being exposed to high levels of androgens before birth might play a role, while other research focuses on the many genes involved.
paragraphs.zh: 專家認為，新名稱將有助於醫師正視患者所面臨的「多系統負擔」。醫學界希望透過擺脫對卵巢的過度關注，讓大眾認識到這是一種複雜的全身性疾病。雖然研究人員仍在研究其確切成因，但他們認為這是遺傳與環境因素共同作用的結果。一些研究顯示，出生前暴露於高濃度的雄性素中可能扮演了一定角色，而其他研究則聚焦於涉及的多種基因。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the shift to PMOS is about providing better care. By using a name that accurately describes the hormonal and metabolic nature of the illness, experts hope to reduce the confusion that many patients like Mavrikis have faced for years. As the medical community adopts this new term, the goal is to ensure that patients receive faster, more accurate diagnoses and treatments that address the full range of their symptoms rather than just one organ.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根結底，改稱為 PMOS 是為了提供更好的照護。透過使用能準確描述該疾病荷爾蒙與代謝本質的名稱，專家希望減少像馬夫里基斯這樣多年來一直面臨困惑的患者數量。隨著醫學界採用這個新術語，目標是確保患者能獲得更快速、更準確的診斷，並接受能解決其所有症狀，而不僅僅是針對單一器官的治療。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A word or phrase used to describe or categorize a person or thing.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（貼標籤），這裡作名詞用，指稱呼或名稱。文中：The name change is not just a simple label update; it is a major effort to change how doctors and patients understand the illness.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To manage, cope with, or experience a particular situation or problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語在文中指患者「患有」或「面臨」某種健康狀況，而非單純的「處理」。文中：Furthermore, about 85% of people with the condition also deal with insulin resistance.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A heavy load or a difficult situation that causes worry or hardship.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處指疾病對身體造成的整體壓力與影響，而非實體的重量。文中：Experts believe that the new name will help doctors see the "multi-system burden" that patients face.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: What is PCOS, what are the symptoms and treatment, and why is it being renamed PMOS?

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: ‘Unprecedented’ global effort gives new name to polycystic ovary syndrome – and new hope to millions of women

quiz.question: What is the primary reason that the appearance of the ovaries in patients with this condition is often misunderstood?

options.A: The ovaries are physically damaged by painful, fluid-filled sacs.
options.B: Immature egg-containing structures are mistaken for cysts.
options.C: The ovaries shrink significantly due to a lack of insulin.
options.D: Doctors are unable to see the ovaries clearly using ultrasound technology.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，教授 Colin Duncan 解釋說，超音波上看到的其實是「卵泡」(follicles) 而非「囊腫」(cysts)，這些卵泡因發育不全而滯留在卵巢中，導致外觀看起來像囊腫。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章指出囊腫並非真實存在；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章並未提到卵巢萎縮；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章提到醫生確實能透過超音波觀察到卵泡，問題在於解讀錯誤。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what is a likely consequence of the previous name, PCOS, for patients?

options.A: Patients were more likely to receive early treatment for heart disease.
options.B: Patients were often misinformed about the nature of their symptoms.
options.C: Patients were less likely to be diagnosed with insulin resistance.
options.D: Patients were able to easily manage their condition through diet alone.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。從文章第五段 Maddy Mavrikis 的案例可知，舊名稱導致醫生過度關注卵巢，甚至對患者做出錯誤的生育預測，並忽略了患者真正的荷爾蒙問題。選項 A 錯誤，因為舊名稱導致診斷延遲，而非提早治療；選項 C 錯誤，文章並未暗示名稱影響了胰島素阻抗的診斷率；選項 D 錯誤，文章強調這是一種複雜的全身性疾病，並非單靠飲食就能輕易解決。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'multi-system burden' refer to?

options.A: The financial cost of managing multiple medical clinics.
options.B: The complex range of health issues affecting various body systems.
options.C: The administrative workload placed on doctors by the new name.
options.D: The physical weight gain caused by high levels of insulin.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到該疾病不僅是卵巢問題，還涉及內分泌系統、代謝系統（如胰島素阻抗）及全身性症狀，因此「multi-system burden」意指該疾病對患者多個生理系統造成的負擔。選項 A 與行政成本無關；選項 C 是對「burden」的誤讀，文中指的負擔是患者的健康狀況而非行政工作；選項 D 過於狹隘，體重增加只是該症狀群的一部分，而非「多系統」的完整定義。

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of renaming PCOS to PMOS?

options.A: To simplify the medical terminology for the general public.
options.B: To highlight that the condition is caused primarily by environmental factors.
options.C: To reflect the condition's nature as a broad hormonal and metabolic disorder.
options.D: To replace outdated medical research with new genetic discoveries.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章的核心主旨在於說明舊名稱過於狹隘（僅關注卵巢），而新名稱 PMOS 更能精確反映其作為「內分泌與代謝」疾病的本質，以改善診斷與治療。選項 A 錯誤，PMOS 並未比 PCOS 更簡單；選項 B 錯誤，文章提到成因是基因與環境的混合，並未強調環境因素是唯一主因；選項 D 錯誤，改名是為了修正理解誤區，而非取代研究。
